cost_of_floor.interpretation: If max_post_fee_pnl_pct_total <= 0, no slice in the corpus produces post-fee positive results — relaxing the gate cannot admit a profitable slice and any reduction in the floor would merely admit losing slices into the live trader.

verdict.rationale: Every per-coin slice in the corpus has post-fee PnL <= 0 (max=-342.9836, n=9). No relaxation of the directional-accuracy floor can admit a profitable slice; the no-weakening hard rule rejects every `is_relaxation_vs_current=true` candidate by definition. Tightening would not change the (already zero) admitted set, so no `adjust_gate` upward is justified either.
